title: senecio pauperculus subsp. crawfordii
intro: Senecio pauperculus subsp. crawfordii, also known as Crawford's ragwort, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family and is native to New Zealand. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and providing relief from skin irritations. Senecio pauperculus subsp. crawfordii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
